methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $14.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $3.2B - $39.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Figure AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series C), post-money $39B

data.narrative:
Skild AI is a Series C artificial intelligence and software company founded by Deepak Pathak and Abhinav Gupta, currently valued at $14.0B post-money with $1.4B+ in total raised. The comp universe consists of nine private frontier-tech companies and one public comparable (Palantir, PLTR), spanning robotics, autonomous systems, and AI infrastructure stages from Series B through Series D.

Two triangulation methods apply to this valuation. Stage-progression analysis, using a 1.7x multiplier typical for Series C to next-stage advancement applied to the $14.0B last post-money valuation, implies $23.8B. Peer-median methodology across four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables (Figure AI at $39.0B, Wayve at $8.6B, Physical Intelligence at $5.6B, and Black Forest Labs at $3.2B) yields a median of $7.1B, with a range spanning $3.2B to $39.0B. The dispersion reflects heterogeneity in business model maturity and capital deployment stage within the cohort.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $6.0B (low) to $27.4B (high), with a median of $15.4B. The current $14.0B valuation sits within the range, slightly below median. Key uncertainties include the wide peer dispersion—driven largely by Figure AI's $39.0B outlier positioning—and the early-stage nature of the comp set, where revenue multiples and path-to-profitability remain nascent across most peers.
